---
title: "3-global_implications_models"
output: html_document
date: "2024-11-04"
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}


require(data.table)
require(ggplot2)
require(sf)
library(Hmisc)
library(haven)
library(patchwork)
library(mgcv)
library(dplyr)
library(tidyverse)
library(broom)
`%!in%` = Negate(`%in%`)

```



### Load data

```{r}

global_dat<- readRDS(file = "/mnt/theia/implications/dmed_global_data/Global Implications Datasets/global_dat_ready.rds")


colnames(global_dat)

global_dat %>% filter(!is.na(POP_CHANGE)) %>% summarise(sum(!is.na(GDP_CHANGE)),
                                                        sum(!is.na(GDP_CHANGE))/nrow(.),
                                                        sum(!is.na(POVERTY_CHANGE)),
                                                        sum(!is.na(POVERTY_CHANGE))/nrow(.),
                                                        sum(!is.na(HDI_CHANGE_INT_00)),
                                                        sum(!is.na(HDI_CHANGE_INT_00))/nrow(.),
                                                          sum(!is.na(median_age_DIFF)),
                                                         sum(!is.na(median_age_DIFF))/nrow(.),
                                                        )

```



```{r}


model_vars<- colnames(global_dat %>% select(GDP_CHANGE, HDI_CHANGE_INT_00, 
                                            POVERTY_CHANGE, median_age_DIFF, median_age_CHANGE))

model_vars

```

### By Econ and Urbanicity 



Groupings

```{r}



groupings<- global_dat %>%   
  group_by(ECONOMY, urban) %>% 
  summarise(n=n(), 
           n.countries=length(unique(NAME)), 
           countries = paste(unique(NAME), collapse = ", "))


write_csv(groupings, file = "/home/dmedgyesi/international/model output/summary statistics/grouping_economy_urban.csv")



groupings_income<- global_dat %>%   
  arrange(INCOME_GRP, NAME) %>% 
  group_by(INCOME_GRP) %>% 
  summarise(n=n(), 
           n.countries=length(unique(NAME)), 
           countries = paste(unique(NAME), collapse = ", "))


write_csv(groupings_income, file = "/home/dmedgyesi/international/model output/summary statistics/grouping_income.csv")





pop_by_group<- global_dat %>% group_by(ECONOMY, urban) %>% 
  summarise(n(),
             median=median(POP_CHANGE), 
              mean=mean(POP_CHANGE),
             `0.25`=quantile(POP_CHANGE, 0.75),
            `0.75`=quantile(POP_CHANGE, probs = 0.75, na.rm = TRUE),
            `0.05`=quantile(POP_CHANGE, probs = 0.05, na.rm = TRUE), 
            `0.95`=quantile(POP_CHANGE, probs = 0.95, na.rm = TRUE), 
            min=min(POP_CHANGE, na.rm = T), 
            max=max(POP_CHANGE, na.rm = T))


write_csv(pop_by_group, file = "/home/dmedgyesi/international/model output/summary statistics/population_distr_by_econ_urban.csv")



global_dat<- global_dat %>% mutate(ECON_URBAN=paste0(ECONOMY, ", ", urban))


descriptive_table<- global_dat %>% 
  group_by(ECONOMY, urban) %>% 
  summarise(n=n(), 
            Pop_change=paste0(sprintf("%.2f", median(POP_CHANGE, na.rm =T )), " (", 
                              sprintf("%.2f", quantile(POP_CHANGE, 0.25, na.rm =T )), ", ", 
                                     sprintf("%.2f", quantile(POP_CHANGE, 0.75, na.rm =T )), ")"), 
            GDP_change=paste0(sprintf("%.2f", median(GDP_CHANGE, na.rm =T )), " (", 
                              sprintf("%.2f", quantile(GDP_CHANGE, 0.25, na.rm =T )), ", ", 
                                     sprintf("%.2f", quantile(GDP_CHANGE, 0.75, na.rm =T )), ")"), 
             Median_Age_Diff=paste0(sprintf("%.2f", median(median_age_DIFF, na.rm =T )), " (", 
                              sprintf("%.2f", quantile(median_age_DIFF, 0.25, na.rm =T )), ", ", 
                                     sprintf("%.2f", quantile(median_age_DIFF, 0.75, na.rm =T )), ")"), 
             Poverty_change=paste0(sprintf("%.3f", median(POVERTY_CHANGE, na.rm =T )), " (", 
                              sprintf("%.3f", quantile(POVERTY_CHANGE, 0.25, na.rm =T )), ", ", 
                                     sprintf("%.3f", quantile(POVERTY_CHANGE, 0.75, na.rm =T )), ")"),
            HDI_change=paste0(sprintf("%.2f", median(HDI_CHANGE_INT_00, na.rm =T )), " (", 
                              sprintf("%.2f", quantile(HDI_CHANGE_INT_00, 0.25, na.rm =T )), ", ", 
                                     sprintf("%.2f", quantile(HDI_CHANGE_INT_00, 0.75, na.rm =T )), ")")) %>% 
  arrange(ECONOMY, desc(urban))


write_csv(descriptive_table, file = "/home/dmedgyesi/international/model output/summary statistics/table1_descriptive.csv")


#overall


global_dat %>% 
  summarise(n=n(), 
            Pop_change=paste0(sprintf("%.2f", median(POP_CHANGE, na.rm =T )), " (", 
                              sprintf("%.2f", quantile(POP_CHANGE, 0.25, na.rm =T )), ", ", 
                                     sprintf("%.2f", quantile(POP_CHANGE, 0.75, na.rm =T )), ")"), 
            GDP_change=paste0(sprintf("%.2f", median(GDP_CHANGE, na.rm =T )), " (", 
                              sprintf("%.2f", quantile(GDP_CHANGE, 0.25, na.rm =T )), ", ", 
                                     sprintf("%.2f", quantile(GDP_CHANGE, 0.75, na.rm =T )), ")"), 
             Median_Age_Diff=paste0(sprintf("%.2f", median(median_age_DIFF, na.rm =T )), " (", 
                              sprintf("%.2f", quantile(median_age_DIFF, 0.25, na.rm =T )), ", ", 
                                     sprintf("%.2f", quantile(median_age_DIFF, 0.75, na.rm =T )), ")"), 
             Poverty_change=paste0(sprintf("%.3f", median(POVERTY_CHANGE, na.rm =T )), " (", 
                              sprintf("%.3f", quantile(POVERTY_CHANGE, 0.25, na.rm =T )), ", ", 
                                     sprintf("%.3f", quantile(POVERTY_CHANGE, 0.75, na.rm =T )), ")"),
            HDI_change=paste0(sprintf("%.2f", median(HDI_CHANGE_INT_00, na.rm =T )), " (", 
                              sprintf("%.2f", quantile(HDI_CHANGE_INT_00, 0.25, na.rm =T )), ", ", 
                                     sprintf("%.2f", quantile(HDI_CHANGE_INT_00, 0.75, na.rm =T )), ")")) 

```



#### LINEAR MODELS


with random effect

```{r}

variables <- model_vars

# Split dataset by ECONOMY instead of ECON_URBAN
economy_groups <- split(global_dat, global_dat$ECONOMY)

# Create an empty list to store results (grouped by variable)
linear_results_list <- list()

# Loop through each dependent variable (store all ECONOMY groups together)
for (var in variables) {
  
  # Create a list to store results for all ECONOMY groups
  econ_results <- list()
  
  # Loop through each ECONOMY class
  for (economy in names(economy_groups)) {
    
    # Subset data
    data_subset <- economy_groups[[economy]]
    
    # Define model formula with urban as an interaction term
    formula <- as.formula(paste(var, "~ POP_CHANGE * urban"))  
    
    # Run mixed-effects model with REGION_WB as a random effect
    model <- lme(fixed = formula, random = ~ 1 | REGION_WB, 
                 data = data_subset, method = "REML", na.action = na.exclude, 
                 control = lmeControl(opt = "optim"))
    
    # Extract model summary manually
    model_summary <- as.data.frame(summary(model)$tTable)  # Extract fixed effects
    model_summary$term <- rownames(model_summary)  # Store term names
    colnames(model_summary) <- c("estimate", "std.error", "DF", "t.value", "p.value", "term")
    
    # Extract estimates
    pop_change <- model_summary %>% filter(term == "POP_CHANGE") %>% select(estimate, std.error, p.value)
    pop_change_urbanT <- model_summary %>% filter(term == "POP_CHANGE:urbanT") %>% select(estimate, std.error, p.value)
    
    # Compute the estimate for urban = TRUE
    urban_estimate <- pop_change$estimate + pop_change_urbanT$estimate
    urban_se <- sqrt(pop_change$std.error^2 + pop_change_urbanT$std.error^2 + 2 * vcov(model)["POP_CHANGE", "POP_CHANGE:urbanT"])
    urban_p_value <- pop_change_urbanT$p.value  # Keep interaction term p-value
    
    # Compute 95% Confidence Intervals
    rural_lower <- pop_change$estimate - 1.96 * pop_change$std.error
    rural_upper <- pop_change$estimate + 1.96 * pop_change$std.error
    urban_lower <- urban_estimate - 1.96 * urban_se
    urban_upper <- urban_estimate + 1.96 * urban_se
    
    # Combine results into a dataframe
    model_results <- data.frame(
      variable = var,
      ECONOMY = economy,
      urban = c("F", "T"),
      estimate = c(pop_change$estimate, urban_estimate),
      std.error = c(pop_change$std.error, urban_se),
      p_value = c(pop_change$p.value, urban_p_value),
      lower = c(rural_lower, urban_lower),
      upper = c(rural_upper, urban_upper)
    )
    
    model_results<- model_results %>% mutate(ECON_URBAN=paste0(ECONOMY, ", ", urban))
    
    # Store the results for this ECONOMY group
    econ_results[[economy]] <- model_results
  }
  
  # Combine all ECONOMY results for this variable into a single dataframe
  linear_results_list[[var]] <- bind_rows(econ_results)
}

# Convert all results into a single dataframe and round all numeric values to 4 decimals
linear_results_df <- bind_rows(linear_results_list, .id = "variable") %>% 
  mutate_if(is.numeric, round, 4) %>% select(ECON_URBAN, ECONOMY, urban, variable, everything())

# View the final combined dataframe
print(linear_results_df)


write_csv(linear_results_df, "/home/dmedgyesi/international/model output/coefficient tables/POP_CHANGE_MEVs_grid_data_coefficients.csv")

# Display first element of results list
linear_results_list[[2]]




```




Plot linear coefficient

```{r}

 
linear_results_df <- linear_results_df %>%
        mutate(ECONOMY = factor(ECONOMY, levels = rev(unique(ECONOMY)))) # Reverse order
 
 
# Get unique variables
variables <- unique(linear_results_df$variable)


var_label<- c("GDP", "Human Development Index", "Poverty", "Median Age", "Median Age")

var_map <- setNames(var_label, variables)



# Create an empty list to store plots
plot_list <- list()

# Loop through each variable
for (var in variables) {
  
  var_label_i <- var_map[var]
  
  unit <- if (var == "median_age_DIFF") {
    "Difference (years)"
  } else {
    "Change"
  }
  
  plot <- linear_results_df %>%
    filter(variable == var) %>%
    ggplot(aes(x = estimate, y = ECONOMY, color = urban, shape = urban)) +
    geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "black") + # Vertical line at zero
    geom_point(position = position_dodge(width = 0.5), size = 3) + # Point estimates
    geom_errorbarh(aes(xmin = lower, xmax = upper), 
                   position = position_dodge(width = 0.5), height = 0.2) + # 95% CI bars
    theme_minimal() +
    labs(title = paste("Population Change –", var_label_i, unit),
         x = paste("Linear coefficient (95% CI)"),
         y = "",
         color = "Urban") +
    theme(legend.position = "bottom",
          panel.grid.major.y = element_blank(), # Remove horizontal gridlines
          panel.grid.minor.y = element_blank()) +
    scale_shape_manual(values = c(16, 17), labels = c("Rural", "Urban"))+  # for shape
    scale_color_manual(values = c("coral", "turquoise"), labels = c("Rural", "Urban"))+  # for color
    guides(color = guide_legend(""), shape = guide_legend(""))
  
  # Store the plot
  plot_list[[var]] <- plot
  
  file_name <- paste0("/home/dmedgyesi/international/model output/plots/coefficient plots/", var, "_BY_ECON_URBAN", "_plot.png")  
  ggsave(file_name, plot = plot, width = 8, height = 9, dpi = 300)
}

# Display one of the plots
print(plot_list)  # Show the first plot





```


Linear results as a table 


```{r}

#create table

#stratification variable must be a factor

global_dat<- global_dat %>% mutate(ECON_URBAN=factor(ECON_URBAN))

group_levels <- unique(global_dat$ECON_URBAN)


#create output dataframe 
# Initialize an empty list to store results for each group
data_list <- list()

# Loop over each economy group and calculate the sequence of percentiles
for (group in group_levels) {
  # Filter data for the current economy group
  group_data <- global_dat %>% filter(ECON_URBAN == group)
  
  # Calculate the 5th and 95th percentiles for population change
  lower <- -0.5 #quantile(group_data$POP_CHANGE, 0.05, na.rm = TRUE)
  upper <- 1 #quantile(group_data$POP_CHANGE, 0.95, na.rm = TRUE)
  
  # Create a sequence from 5th to 95th percentile with step of 0.01
  poptot_change_seq <- round(seq(lower, upper, by = 0.01),2)
  
  # Create a temporary dataframe for the current group
  temp_data <- data.frame(ECON_URBAN = group, POP_CHANGE = poptot_change_seq)
  
  # Append to the list
  data_list[[group]] <- temp_data
}

# Combine all dataframes into one
new_data <- rbindlist(data_list)

# View the result
head(new_data)

sep.cats<- global_dat %>% 
  group_by(ECON_URBAN, ECONOMY, urban) %>% 
  slice(1) %>% select(ECON_URBAN, ECONOMY, urban) %>% ungroup()

new_data<- new_data %>% left_join(sep.cats, by="ECON_URBAN") %>% 
  select(ECON_URBAN, ECONOMY,urban, everything())

new_data<- new_data %>% mutate(urban=as.character(urban))

new_data<- new_data %>% arrange(ECON_URBAN, POP_CHANGE)

new_data<- new_data %>% select(-c(ECONOMY, urban))

```

```{r}


variables<- names(linear_results_list)

var_label<- c("GDP", "Human Development Index", "Poverty", "Median Age", "Median Age")

var_map <- setNames(var_label, variables)



# Create an empty list to store plots
plot_list_continuous <- list()

# Loop through each element in the list (each variable's results)
for (var in variables) {
  
  #set labels
  var_label_i <- var_map[var]
  
  unit <- if (var == "median_age_DIFF") {
    "Difference (years)"
  } else {
    "Change"
  }
  
  unit_y <- if (var == "median_age_DIFF") {
    "Difference (years)"
  } else {
    "Change (%)"
  }
  
  
  # Merge data with `new_data`
  linear_table <- new_data %>% left_join(linear_results_list[[var]], by="ECON_URBAN")
  

   
  # Compute change estimates
  linear_table <- linear_table %>%
    mutate(change = estimate * POP_CHANGE,
           change_se = std.error * abs(POP_CHANGE),
           change_lower = change - (1.96 * change_se),
           change_upper = change + (1.96 * change_se))
  
  #convert to percentage except median age difference
estimates <- c("change","change_lower","change_upper")

 linear_table <- if (var == "median_age_DIFF") {
    linear_table
  } else {
    linear_table %>% 
  mutate(across(all_of(estimates), 
                ~ (100*.), 
                .names = "{.col}"))
  }


  
  # Generate the plot for this variable
  plot <- ggplot(data = linear_table) +
    geom_hline(yintercept = 0, col = "darkgrey", linetype = "dashed") +  
    geom_vline(xintercept = 0, col = "darkgrey", linetype = "dashed") +  
    geom_line(aes(x = 100*(POP_CHANGE), y = (change), color = urban)) +
    geom_ribbon(aes(x = 100*(POP_CHANGE), ymin = (change_lower), ymax = (change_upper), fill = urban), 
                alpha = 0.5) +
    theme_minimal() +
    facet_wrap(~ECONOMY, ncol = 2, scales = "free") + 
    scale_x_continuous(limits = c(-50, 100)) +
    labs(title = paste("Population Change –", var_label_i, unit),
               x = "Population Change (%)",
               y = paste(var_label_i, unit_y),
               fill="", 
               color="") +
    scale_color_manual(values = c("coral", "turquoise"), labels = c("Rural", "Urban"))+ 
    scale_fill_manual(values = c("coral", "turquoise"), labels = c("Rural", "Urban"))+ 
    theme(legend.position = "bottom")
  
  # Store plot in list with variable name
  plot_list_continuous[[var]] <- plot
   
  file_name <- paste0("/home/dmedgyesi/international/model output/plots/continuous plots/", var, "_BY_ECON_URBAN", "_plot.png")  
  ggsave(file_name, plot = plot, width = 8, height = 9, dpi = 300)
  
}

# Display one of the plots
print(plot_list_continuous)  # Show first plot


 
 
 

```


For journal
```{r}
library(patchwork)

combined <- plot_list[[1]] + plot_list_continuous[[1]]+ plot_layout(widths = c(1, 1.3))

combined

ggsave("/home/dmedgyesi/international/model output/plots/Fig3.png", width = 12, height = 7, dpi = 300)


ggsave("/home/dmedgyesi/international/model output/plots/Fig3.eps", width = 12, height = 7)


```



#### GAM MODELS - SPLINES

Prep data

```{r}


#stratification variable must be a factor

global_dat<- global_dat %>% mutate(ECON_URBAN=factor(ECON_URBAN))

group_levels <- unique(global_dat$ECON_URBAN)


#create output dataframe 
# Initialize an empty list to store results for each group
data_list <- list()

# Loop over each economy group and calculate the sequence of percentiles
for (group in group_levels) {
  # Filter data for the current economy group
  group_data <- global_dat %>% filter(ECON_URBAN == group)
  
  # Calculate the 5th and 95th percentiles for population change
  lower <- -1 #quantile(group_data$POP_CHANGE, 0.05, na.rm = TRUE)
  upper <- 2  #quantile(group_data$POP_CHANGE, 0.95, na.rm = TRUE)
  
  # Create a sequence from 5th to 95th percentile with step of 0.01
  poptot_change_seq <- round(seq(lower, upper, by = 0.01),2)
  
  # Create a temporary dataframe for the current group
  temp_data <- data.frame(ECON_URBAN = group, POP_CHANGE = poptot_change_seq)
  
  # Append to the list
  data_list[[group]] <- temp_data
}

# Combine all dataframes into one
new_data <- rbindlist(data_list)

# View the result
head(new_data)

sep.cats<- global_dat %>% 
  group_by(ECON_URBAN, ECONOMY, urban) %>% 
  slice(1) %>% select(ECON_URBAN, ECONOMY, urban) %>% ungroup()

new_data<- new_data %>% left_join(sep.cats, by="ECON_URBAN") %>% 
  select(ECON_URBAN, ECONOMY,urban, everything())

new_data<- new_data %>% mutate(urban=as.character(urban))

```

Run and export models 

```{r}



models.str <- list()

for (name in model_vars) {
  # Fit the model
  formula_str <- paste(name, " ~ s(POP_CHANGE, bs='cr', k=3, by=ECON_URBAN)", 
                       sep = "")
  formula_obj <- as.formula(formula_str)
  model <- gam(formula_obj, data = global_dat, family = gaussian)
  
  # Predict using the model and include standard errors for confidence intervals
  preds <- predict(model, newdata = new_data, type = "response", se.fit = TRUE)
  mod_df<- new_data
  mod_df$predicted <- as.vector(preds$fit)
  mod_df$se <- as.vector(preds$se.fit)
  # Calculate 95% confidence intervals
  ci <- as.vector(1.96 * preds$se.fit)
  mod_df$upper_pred <- mod_df$predicted + ci
  mod_df$lower_pred <- mod_df$predicted - ci
  #Subtract the predicted value at pop_change=0 (i.e., center value)  
  center.val<- mod_df %>% filter(POP_CHANGE==0) %>% 
    select(ECON_URBAN, predicted) %>% 
    rename(center=predicted)
  mod_df<-mod_df %>% left_join(center.val, by="ECON_URBAN")
  mod_df<- mod_df %>% mutate(change=predicted-center)
  mod_df$upper_change <- mod_df$change + ci
  mod_df$lower_change <- mod_df$change - ci
  mod_df<- mod_df %>% mutate_if(is.numeric, round, 2)
  mod_df<- mod_df %>% mutate(var.type=name) %>% select(var.type, everything())
  models.str[[name]] <- mod_df
  
  file_name <- paste0("/home/dmedgyesi/international/model output/", 
                      name,"_BY_ECON_URBAN", ".csv")
  write_csv(mod_df, file_name)

}


look<- models.str[[1]]

```


Plot results

```{r}

plots.str <- list()

for (name in names(models.str)) {
     suppressWarnings({
  #pick a reasonable y limits 
    dat_limits<- models.str[[name]]  
    y_low <- quantile(dat_limits$lower_change, 0.02,na.rm = TRUE)
    y_high <- quantile(dat_limits$upper_change, 0.98,na.rm = TRUE)
    avg_change_per_group <- dat_limits %>%
            group_by(ECONOMY, urban) %>%
            summarise(avg_change = median(change, na.rm = TRUE)) %>% ungroup()
               
     
  
    p <- ggplot(data = models.str[[name]]) +
          geom_hline(yintercept = 0, col = "darkgrey", linetype = "dashed") +  
          geom_vline(xintercept = 0, col = "darkgrey", linetype = "dashed") +  
          geom_line(aes(x = POP_CHANGE, y = change, color=urban)) +
          geom_ribbon(aes(x = POP_CHANGE, ymin = lower_change, ymax = upper_change, fill=urban), 
                alpha = 0.5) +
    theme_minimal() +
    facet_wrap(~ECONOMY, ncol=2, scales = "free") +
    scale_x_continuous(limits = c(-1, 2)) +
    #scale_y_continuous(limits = c(y_low, y_high))  +
    labs(title = paste(name)) 
    
  plots.str[[name]] <-p  
  print(plots.str[[name]])
  
  # Save the plot
  file_name <- paste0("/home/dmedgyesi/international/model output/plots/", name, "_BY_ECON_URBAN", "_plot.png")  
  ggsave(file_name, plot = p, width = 8, height = 9, dpi = 300)
     })
}



```





### By Economy 

Groupings

```{r}


groupings<- global_dat %>%   
  group_by(ECONOMY) %>% 
  summarise(n=n(), 
           n.countries=length(unique(NAME)), 
           countries = paste(unique(NAME), collapse = ", "))


write_csv(groupings, file = "/home/dmedgyesi/international/output/grouping_economy.csv")


pop_by_group<- global_dat %>% group_by(ECONOMY) %>% 
  summarise(n(),
            `0.05`=quantile(POP_CHANGE, probs = 0.05, na.rm = TRUE), 
            `0.95`=quantile(POP_CHANGE, probs = 0.95, na.rm = TRUE), 
            min=min(POP_CHANGE, na.rm = T), 
            max=max(POP_CHANGE, na.rm = T), 
            median=median(POP_CHANGE), 
            `0.25`=quantile(POP_CHANGE, 0.25))





```




Prep data

```{r}


#stratification variable must be a factor

global_dat<- global_dat %>% mutate(ECONOMY=factor(ECONOMY))

group_levels <- unique(global_dat$ECONOMY)


#create output dataframe 
# Initialize an empty list to store results for each group
data_list <- list()

# Loop over each economy group and calculate the sequence of percentiles
for (group in group_levels) {
  # Filter data for the current economy group
  group_data <- global_dat %>% filter(ECONOMY == group)
  
  # Calculate the 5th and 95th percentiles for population change
  lower <- quantile(group_data$POP_CHANGE, 0.05, na.rm = TRUE)
  upper <- quantile(group_data$POP_CHANGE, 0.95, na.rm = TRUE)
  
  # Create a sequence from 5th to 95th percentile with step of 0.01
  poptot_change_seq <- round(seq(lower, upper, by = 0.01),2)
  
  # Create a temporary dataframe for the current group
  temp_data <- data.frame(ECONOMY = group, POP_CHANGE = poptot_change_seq)
  
  # Append to the list
  data_list[[group]] <- temp_data
}

# Combine all dataframes into one
new_data <- rbindlist(data_list)

# View the result
head(new_data)






```

Run and export models 

```{r}

models.str <- list()

for (name in model_vars) {
  # Fit the model
  formula_str <- paste(name, " ~ s(POP_CHANGE, bs='cr', k=3, by=ECONOMY)", 
                       sep = "")
  formula_obj <- as.formula(formula_str)
  model <- gam(formula_obj, data = global_dat, family = gaussian)
  
  # Predict using the model and include standard errors for confidence intervals
  preds <- predict(model, newdata = new_data, type = "response", se.fit = TRUE)
  mod_df<- new_data
  mod_df$predicted <- as.vector(preds$fit)
  mod_df$se <- as.vector(preds$se.fit)
  # Calculate 95% confidence intervals
  ci <- as.vector(1.96 * preds$se.fit)
  mod_df$upper_pred <- mod_df$predicted + ci
  mod_df$lower_pred <- mod_df$predicted - ci
  #Subtract the predicted value at pop_change=0 (i.e., center value)  
  center.val<- mod_df %>% filter(POP_CHANGE==0) %>% 
    select(ECONOMY, predicted) %>% 
    rename(center=predicted)
  mod_df<-mod_df %>% left_join(center.val, by="ECONOMY")
  mod_df<- mod_df %>% mutate(change=predicted-center)
  mod_df$upper_change <- mod_df$change + ci
  mod_df$lower_change <- mod_df$change - ci
  mod_df<- mod_df %>% mutate_if(is.numeric, round, 2)
  mod_df<- mod_df %>% mutate(var.type=name) %>% select(var.type, everything())
  models.str[[name]] <- mod_df
  
  file_name <- paste0("/home/dmedgyesi/international/model output/", 
                      name,"_BY_ECONOMY", ".csv")
  write_csv(mod_df, file_name)

}


```


Plot results

```{r}

plots.str <- list()

for (name in names(models.str)) {
     suppressWarnings({
  #pick a reasonable y limits 
    dat_limits<- models.str[[name]]  
    y_low <- quantile(dat_limits$lower_change, 0.02,na.rm = TRUE)
    y_high <- quantile(dat_limits$upper_change, 0.98,na.rm = TRUE)
    
  
    p <- ggplot(data = models.str[[name]]) +
          geom_hline(aes(yintercept = 0), 
                       col = "darkgrey", 
                       linetype = "dashed") +
      geom_vline(xintercept = 0, col = "darkgrey", linetype = "dashed") +  
    geom_line(aes(x = POP_CHANGE, y = change, color=ECONOMY)) +
    geom_ribbon(aes(x = POP_CHANGE, ymin = lower_change, ymax = upper_change, fill=ECONOMY), 
                alpha = 0.5) +
    theme_minimal() +
    theme(legend.position = "none")+
    facet_wrap(~ECONOMY, ncol=2) +
    scale_x_continuous(limits = c(-0.6, 2)) +
    scale_y_continuous(limits = c(y_low, y_high))  +
    labs(title = paste(name)) 
    
  plots.str[[name]] <-p  
  print(plots.str[[name]])
  
  # Save the plot
  file_name <- paste0("/home/dmedgyesi/international/model output/plots/", name, "_BY_ECONOMY", "_plot.png")  
  ggsave(file_name, plot = p, width = 8, height = 9, dpi = 300)
     })
}



```


#### EXTRA 


linear no random effect
```{r}
global_dat<- global_dat %>% mutate(INCOME_URBAN=paste0(INCOME_GRP, ", ", urban))

# Split dataset by ECONOMY class
economy_groups <- c(split(global_dat, global_dat$INCOME_URBAN))


# Create an empty list to store results by variable
linear_results_list <- list()

# Loop through each dependent variable (model_vars)
for (var in model_vars) {
  
  # Create an empty list to store results for each economy
  economy_results <- list()
  
  # Loop through each ECONOMY class
  for (economy in names(economy_groups)) {
    
    data_subset <- economy_groups[[economy]]
    
    # Run linear model
    formula <- as.formula(paste(var, "~ POP_CHANGE + ISO_A3"))
    model <- lm(formula, data = data_subset)
    
    # Extract model summary using broom and exclude intercept
    model_summary <- tidy(model) %>% 
      filter(term == "POP_CHANGE") %>%
      mutate(
        lower = estimate - 1.96 * std.error,  # 95% CI lower bound
        upper = estimate + 1.96 * std.error,  # 95% CI upper bound
        variable = var,  # Store the variable name
        INCOME_URBAN = economy  # Store the economy name
      ) %>%
      select(variable, INCOME_URBAN, estimate, std.error, p.value, lower, upper)  # Keep generic column names
    
    
    
    # Store results for this economy
    economy_results[[economy]] <- model_summary
  }
  
  # Combine all economy results for this variable into a single dataframe
  linear_results_list[[var]] <- bind_rows(economy_results)
}


# If you want to combine everything into a single dataframe:
linear_results_df <- bind_rows(linear_results_list, .id = "variable")

linear_results_df <- linear_results_df %>% 
  mutate_if(is.numeric, round, 4)



```

linear urban interactive term

```{r}


# Split dataset by ECONOMY instead of ECON_URBAN
economy_groups <- split(global_dat, global_dat$ECONOMY)

# Create an empty list to store results by variable
linear_results_list <- list()

# Loop through each dependent variable (model_vars)
for (var in model_vars) {
  
  # Create an empty list to store results for each economy
  economy_results <- list()
  
  # Loop through each ECONOMY class
  for (economy in names(economy_groups)) {
    
    data_subset <- economy_groups[[economy]]
    
    # Define model formula with urban as an interaction term
    formula <- as.formula(paste(var, "~ POP_CHANGE * urban"))  
    model <- lm(formula, data = data_subset)
    
    # Extract model summary using broom
    model_summary <- tidy(model)
    
    # Extract estimates for rural and urban effects
    pop_change <- model_summary %>% filter(term == "POP_CHANGE") %>% select(estimate, std.error, p.value)
    pop_change_urbanT <- model_summary %>% filter(term == "POP_CHANGE:urbanT") %>% select(estimate, std.error, p.value)
    
    # Compute the estimate for urban = TRUE
    urban_estimate <- pop_change$estimate + pop_change_urbanT$estimate
    
    # Compute the correct standard error for urban
    cov_matrix <- vcov(model)
    urban_se <- sqrt(pop_change$std.error^2 + pop_change_urbanT$std.error^2 + 2 * cov_matrix["POP_CHANGE", "POP_CHANGE:urbanT"])
    urban_p_value <- pop_change_urbanT$p.value  # Keep interaction term p-value
    
    # Compute 95% Confidence Intervals
    rural_lower <- pop_change$estimate - 1.96 * pop_change$std.error
    rural_upper <- pop_change$estimate + 1.96 * pop_change$std.error
    urban_lower <- urban_estimate - 1.96 * urban_se
    urban_upper <- urban_estimate + 1.96 * urban_se
    
    # Combine results into a dataframe
    model_results <- data.frame(
      variable = var,
      ECONOMY = economy,
      urban = c("F", "T"),
      estimate = c(pop_change$estimate, urban_estimate),
      std.error = c(pop_change$std.error, urban_se),
      p_value = c(pop_change$p.value, urban_p_value),
      lower = c(rural_lower, urban_lower),
      upper = c(rural_upper, urban_upper)
    )
    
    # Create a combined column for ECONOMY and urban category
    model_results <- model_results %>% mutate(ECON_URBAN = paste0(ECONOMY, ", ", urban))
    
    # Store the results for this ECONOMY group
    economy_results[[economy]] <- model_results
  }
  
  # Combine all ECONOMY results for this variable into a single dataframe
  linear_results_list[[var]] <- bind_rows(economy_results)
}

# Convert all results into a single dataframe and round all numeric values to 4 decimals
linear_results_df <- bind_rows(linear_results_list, .id = "variable") %>% 
  mutate_if(is.numeric, round, 4)

# View the final combined dataframe
print(linear_results_df)

# Display first element of results list
linear_results_list[[1]]



```
